def: s.f. Welfare, safety; happiness; — intj. Peace be to you! all hail!

def: s.m. Consumption, pthisis pulmonalis.

s.m. A tailor; a seamster.

subs: (-meṅ?), The itch to break out, to have the itch

def: s.m. Food; — a kind of sweetmeat (like pie-crust).
s.m. Shame, &c. ( = ḵẖijālat, q.v.).

def: s.f. Bashfulness, shame, sense of shame.
adj. Ashamed, abashed; bashful, modest.

ety: khājā, 'a kind of sweetmeat'+dim. aff. lā = rā = S. र+कः
def: s.m. = khājā, q.v. s.m. Food; — a kind of sweetmeat (like pie-crust).

ety: for A. خجلة, v.n. fr. خجل

subs: ḵẖijlat ḵẖānā, v.n. To feel or experience a sense of shame, to be bashful; to blush.

def: s.m. = khajūrā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Plaited, matted; twisted, twined (as hair, mats, &c.); — s.m. The ridge of a thatched roof; — a centipede, Scolopendra (cf. kan-khajūrā).

ety: Prk. खज्जूरो, or खज्जूरं; S. खर्जूरः, or खर्जूरं
def: s.f. A kind of silk cloth stained with waving marks like the traces of the old leaves on the trunk of the date-tree
subs: s.m. The juice or extract of the wild date (tāṛī), used (when in a state of fermentation) to leaven bread, and as an intoxicating liquor.
ex: ہر صبح کھجور اور دودھ صحت کے لئے اچھا ہوتا ہے
har subah khajūr aur dūdh sehat ke lie acchā hotā hai
Dates and milk every morning is good for health
wety: Inherited from Prakrit खज्जूर (khajjūra), from Sanskrit खर्जूर (kharjūra). Cognate with Gujarati ખજૂર (khajūr), Marathi खजूर (khajūr), Punjabi ਖਜੂਰ / کَھجُور (khajūr), Bengali খেজুর (khejur), Assamese খেজুৰ (khezur).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Plaited, matted; twisted, twined (as hair, mats, &c.); — s.m. The ridge of a thatched roof; — a centipede, Scolopendra (cf. kan-khajūrā).
adj. = khajūrā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Plaited, matted; twisted, twined (as hair, mats, &c.); — s.m. The ridge of a thatched roof; — a centipede, Scolopendra (cf. kan-khajūrā).

s.m. A kind of rattle used in breaking in a horse (it is formed of a small board, shaped somewhat like a bleacher's beetle or mall, on each side of the flat part of which three or four rows of rattles are fixed and suspended; by an occasional shake of this, one of the horsebreaker's assistants who follows the horse with it in hand, helps to break in the horse, and to give him his proper paces).
s.f. Hurrying; bustle; labour, difficulty, trouble, trial
v.n. To hurry, to bustle to and fro; to use strenuous effort.
s.f. A hollow in a tree, &c.).

subs: s.m. An addled egg, wind egg; a term of reproach; bastard.

subs: adj. & adv. Thick, close, thickly spread; — little, scarce, rare; — very few, few and far between, here and there; rarely.

ety: inf. n. of خلع 'to pull or throw off,' &c.
def: s.m. Pulling off, stripping off, stripping; deposing, removing (from office)
subs: lit. 'He has thrown off the rein'; is unchecked or uncontrolled.

def: s.f. (m.?) A creek, bay, gulf; inlet (of the sea, or of a large river); — a canal, rivulet, watercourse, river; — a moat, trench.

ety: S. खल्लः(?); cf. ćhāl = S. शल्कं
def: s.f. Skin, hide; — bellows
subs: s.m. 'One who takes the skin off'; one who is exacting in money-matters; a skin-flint
or khāl udheṛnā, or khāl uṛānā (-kī), To take the skin off, to flay.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Low, base, mean, vile: malicious, mischievous; artful, bad, wicked; cruel; — a wicked or mischievous person, &c.; — s.m. A threshing-floor; a granary; a stock (of grain); a pit; — an entertainment; — deposit of oil, sediment, the cake that remains after the oil has been pressed out of certain seeds (esp. sesamum and sarsoṅ), oil-cake (com. khalī or kharī); — the plant Achyranthes lanata, the woolly Illecebrum or mountain knot-grass
subs: (˚la+uk˚), a.f. Low or wicked language, abuse

def: s.f. Crash (as of an earthen vessel in breaking to pieces).

ety: inf. n. of خلو 'to be empty or vacant,' &c.
def: adj. = ḵẖālī, q.v.; — s.m. A vacant place or space, vacancy, vacuity, vacuum
subs: adj. & s.m. Unfeigned, sincere; intimate, close (friendship); — sincere or un feigned friendship; warm or close attachment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلاء (xalā'), from Arabic خَلَاء (ḵalāʔ).

def: s.m. A long pole with which a boat is impelled, an oar, a helm, a rudder (syn. patwār); — any sharp piercing thing (as a needle or awl): — wind in the bowels causing acute pain; a pain that comes suddenly in the side and joints; anything that passes away by degrees or slowly; — a written document signed by a judge (confirming any mercantile transaction, &c.); — trifles, idle talk, silly discourse.
s.m. A hole, hollow, pit, cavity: low land; a ravine; a creek, &c. ( = khāl, q.v.).
s.m. A granary, &c. (see khal).

ety: for A. خالة, fem. of ḵẖāl
def: s.f. Maternal aunt, mother's sister
subs: or ḵẖālā-māmā-kā ghar, or ḵẖāna-ě-ḵẖāla, s.m. 'Aunt's house'; a place of rest and security.
